title: Farnham Pirates

infobox.value: were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team located in [Farnham, Quebec](/wiki/Farnham,_Quebec "Farnham, Quebec"), Canada. They played in the [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball_minor_league) "Provincial League (baseball minor league)") from 1948 to 1951.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The team was managed by [Sam Bankhead](/wiki/Sam_Bankhead "Sam Bankhead"), who was a [player-manager](/wiki/Player-coach "Player-coach").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was the first black coach in [Minor League Baseball](/wiki/Minor_League_Baseball "Minor League Baseball").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
["Farnham, QC, Canada Minor League History"](https://www.baseball-reference.com/minors/team.cgi?city=Farnham&state=QC&country=CA). baseball-reference.com. Retrieved June 15, 2012.
["Sam Bankhead Minor League Statistics & History"](https://www.baseball-reference.com/minors/player.cgi?id=bankhe001sam). baseball-reference.com. Retrieved June 15, 2012.

Bjarkman, Peter C. (2005). *Diamonds Around The Globe: The Encyclopedia Of International Baseball*. Greenwood Publishing Group. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0313322686](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0313322686 "Special:BookSources/0313322686").

Hendriks, Andrew. ["July 4, 1948: Thomas first Canuck to break color barrier in pro ball"](https://www.canadianbaseballnetwork.com/canadian-baseball-network-articles//thomas-first-canuck-to-break-color-barrier-68-years-ago?format=amp). *canadianbaseballnetwork.com*. Retrieved November 28, 2019.

first_paragraph: The **Farnham Pirates** were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team located in [Farnham, Quebec](/wiki/Farnham,_Quebec "Farnham, Quebec"), Canada. They played in the [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball_minor_league) "Provincial League (baseball minor league)") from 1948 to 1951.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The team was managed by [Sam Bankhead](/wiki/Sam_Bankhead "Sam Bankhead"), who was a [player-manager](/wiki/Player-coach "Player-coach").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was the first black coach in [Minor League Baseball](/wiki/Minor_League_Baseball "Minor League Baseball").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

The **Farnham Pirates** were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team located in [Farnham, Quebec](/wiki/Farnham,_Quebec "Farnham, Quebec"), Canada. They played in the [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball_minor_league) "Provincial League (baseball minor league)") from 1948 to 1951.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The team was managed by [Sam Bankhead](/wiki/Sam_Bankhead "Sam Bankhead"), who was a [player-manager](/wiki/Player-coach "Player-coach").[[2]](#cite_note-2) He was the first black coach in [Minor League Baseball](/wiki/Minor_League_Baseball "Minor League Baseball").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Outfielder [Fred Thomas](/wiki/Fred_Thomas_(athlete) "Fred Thomas (athlete)") played 58 games in the 1948 season batting .351 which caught the attention of [Major League Baseball](/wiki/Major_League_Baseball "Major League Baseball") scouts. He was selected by the [Cleveland Indians](/wiki/Cleveland_Indians "Cleveland Indians") to join the [Wilkes-Barre Barons](/wiki/Wilkes-Barre_(minor_league_baseball_club) "Wilkes-Barre (minor league baseball club)") [farm-team](/wiki/Farm-team "Farm-team") who played in the [Eastern League](/wiki/Eastern_League_(1938%E2%80%932020) "Eastern League (1938–2020)"), where he was the first black player in the league.[[4]](#cite_note-cbn-4)
